notes: Goal remains <20 ms. The current reproducible exact-base neighborhood is round 69 at 25.49913597 ms; round 72 measured 25.59897614 ms after removing the second export-side syncwarp again, so export-lifetime recheck is also closed-negative as a primary direction in this regime. Current profile still looks compute/schedule limited rather than bandwidth limited: tensor 47.95%, SM throughput 47.47%, warps_active 16.60%, DRAM 10.38%, L2 30.98%, barrier 6.54%, long_scoreboard 4.19%. That makes more barrier/lifetime surgery a weak bet and shifts ranking toward the remaining schedule-shape families. Human-idea audit: tiling 256x128/64x64 remains open only through the auxiliary 256x128 family; coalescing and wide global access are already substantially covered and do not read as primary at 10.38% DRAM; shared-memory reuse is already built into the accepted layouts; async copy and Pg2s double buffer are already present on the 256x128 auxiliary kernel; bank-conflict handling lacks a strong profile signal and stays deferred. L2 cache swizzle and launch-order locality are rejected for this round because snake mapping regressed; register reuse keeps the accepted right-left consume order plus reversed row-pair traversal and rejects further consumer-order sweeps; Ps2r double buffer as extra-live lookahead is closed-negative, so only semantics-preserving helper-shape fallbacks stay open; broad stage or multi-buffer expansion and narrow handoff closure remain rejected.

directions.name: Retune The Auxiliary 256x128 Hot-Band K-Loop Schedule
directions.hypothesis: The exact PTX round-69 surface has exhausted the bounded active-path levers that were still open, and round 72 showed export-side lifetime changes are not a win. The remaining materially different bounded family is the non-PTX 256x128 auxiliary hot-band path, whose local K-loop schedule is still conservative (`#pragma unroll 1`) despite already carrying async-copy staging and larger reuse tiles. A small local retune there, such as increasing only the steady-state unroll or lightly reshaping the per-tile loop, can test whether the larger reuse tile recovers tensor utilization without disturbing the accepted PTX base.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Compute scheduling and latency hiding on the auxiliary 256x128 hot-band path, not DRAM bandwidth.
directions.risk: Medium. This path is auxiliary rather than the current default PTX path, so upside depends on the alternative hot-band route being worth reviving; an over-aggressive unroll can also raise registers or scoreboard stalls. The PTX round-69 base should remain unchanged.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if the isolated 256x128 local schedule retune does not improve runtime from the exact round-69 base neighborhood or if it requires changing the accepted PTX default path semantics.

directions.name: Flatten PTX Hot-Band Accumulate Helpers Without Changing Consume Semantics
directions.hypothesis: If there is still a bounded PTX-side move left, it is not another issue-order experiment. The fallback is to keep the accepted right-left consume order, reversed row-pair traversal, one-sync handoff, and linear export exactly as they are, but reshape the recursive `64x64` PTX accumulate helpers into a flatter helper form so ptxas sees the same work order with simpler live ranges and less template-recursion scaffolding.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register allocation and compiler scheduling friction inside the active PTX hot-band consume path.
directions.risk: Medium-high. The upside is likely smaller than dir_01, and it is easy to accidentally drift back into the already-closed issue-grouping or extra-live lookahead families if the helper rewrite changes ordering or live fragments instead of only code shape.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if the helper flattening changes accepted traversal semantics or fails to improve runtime beyond the exact round-69 base.

directions.name: Flatten PTX Export Helper Shape While Preserving Exact Sync And Linear Order
directions.hypothesis: Round 72 closed export-lifetime trimming itself, but the exact accepted export path may still allow a very low-rank helper-shape cleanup. The only acceptable version now is to preserve both export-side sync points and the original linear export order, while simplifying recursion or hoisting invariant scratch-pointer arithmetic in the PTX export helpers so the compiler emits the same semantics with less instruction overhead or register clutter.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Minor export-side instruction overhead and register pressure, not barrier lifetime.
directions.risk: Low-medium. This is intentionally low rank because export-lifetime already lost, so upside is limited; any accidental sync removal or export-order change would reopen a closed-negative family and invalidate the test.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if the export helper flattening changes sync count or linear export order, or if it stays runtime-flat against the exact round-69 base.
